lexical.notes: Content is heavily aggregated/curated rather than original—posts consist largely of shared articles, video summaries, and quotes about Sam Altman and AI. Platform boilerplate (LinkedIn UI text) dominates the corpus. When original commentary appears, it uses accessible tech/business vocabulary with occasional AI-specific terms.

structural.list_usage: Frequent—numbered lists for quotes, takeaways, or key points are common

examples: 10 quotes from Lex Fridman discussion with Sam Altman OpenAI CEO on GPT-4, ChatGPT and the future of AI. It was a 2 hours 24 minutes long video. I extracted major quotes from it. 1 -The increase in quality of life that AI can deliver is extraordinary, AI can make the world amazing. 2- I am excited in a world where AI is an extension of human will and an amplifier of human abilities. - Sam Altman 3 - What matters is getting the best performance, not the number of parameters and compute. - Sam Altman 4 - Open AI is building in public to discover what the public really want, they intend to make mistakes when the stakes are low and get better and better each time. 5 - We need to come together and debate how to draw boundaries for a system like AGI . 6 - ChatGPT represents a significant moment in AI, not because of the underline models but because of its usability. 7 - ChatGPT and GPT4 is better used as a database instead of a reasoning engine. ChatGPT struggles with some of the things that seem easy, like counting words. 8 - RLHF is how to align model to what humans want it to do. 9 - We can build GPT systems way less bias than human, because there will be no emotional load. 10 - The current worries I have now are disinformation problems, economic shocks, etc these do not require super intelligence. Bonus - Ask GPT4 if GPT4 is conscious? It says NO

Sam Altman on what most startups and VCs are getting wrong about the future of AI "I think there are two fundamental strategies to build an AI startup right now. You either bet the technology is about as good as it's going to be, or you bet that the technology is going to get massively better."

OpenAI CEO Sam Altman received a modest $76,001 in compensation last year, up slightly from $73,546 in 2022, a newly released tax filing shows. Altman, who is worth at least $2 billion, has previously said he gets paid "whatever the minimum for health insurance is." He has also repeatedly said he does not own equity in OpenAI.
I asked Sam Altman: "How do you choose what to work on?" Here's what he told me.

Sam Altman Was Fired From His Own Company —Here's What Every Founder Needs to Know. Sam Altman, the CEO and co-founder of OpenAI —a company recently valued at $90B —was just fired by his board. He's not alone. In fact, over 75% of VC-backed founders are removed from their role as CEO before their company goes public or is acquired.
